key_insight: Length penalties on incorrect answers destabilize GRPO by amplifying divergent advantages, so efficiency training must reward brevity only for correct completions and adaptively regulate compression.
engineering_relevance: ACOER offers a more stable way to cut token use in large reasoning models without sacrificing accuracy, making it useful for cheaper and faster deployment of math reasoning systems.
method: Apply brevity rewards only to correct answers to avoid the structural penalty loop
Use dynamic budget normalization to keep response length from over-compressing
Add control-loop penalty adjustment to prevent stochastic collapse during training
evidence_signal: Across diverse mathematical reasoning benchmarks, the method improved overall accuracy over the base model while reducing token generation by more than 60%.
watch_item: The control-loop and budget settings likely need careful tuning, since overly aggressive compression can still trigger collapse or weaken reasoning.
paper_title: Beyond Penalizing Mistakes: Stabilizing Efficiency Training in Large Reasoning Models via Adaptive Correct-Only Rewards
